page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. This ARM64-based CPU features 4 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:6 vCPU-to-memory ratio, and includes a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD. It runs on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core and single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng scores of 6055.17 and 1519.48 respectively. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, registering at 73.07 GB/sec for read-only operations and 35.12 GB/sec for write operations, though small-block cached reads are weaker at 236.0 GB/sec. Passmark database operations perform at an average level with a score of 2615.53. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in single-threaded compression, software compilation, and LLM inference tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the is4gen.xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe SSD storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for storage-heavy workloads without paying for excess CPU cores. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this instance suitable for database operations, low-latency local storage caching, and general-purpose applications requiring significant local storage capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring a 4-core ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 24.0 GB of memory and a 3750 GB local NVMe SSD, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with a 6 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities and average database operations, alongside weaker performance in software compilation and LLM inference. The instance provides high storage density relative to its compute footprint, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-intensive applications. It is best suited for database hosting, local caching, and storage-heavy workloads.

3750 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for low-latency data access
